title: ApprovalEnvelope
description: The agentq wire format for a single agent permission prompt surrendered to an out-of-band human reviewer. Any agent runtime that emits this shape can POST it to the daemon (POST /api/envelopes) and participate in the same triage queue without going through agentq's stdio wrapper. Making this envelope public is the product's moat: adoption no longer requires agentq's own matchers.

id.description: A ULID assigned by the producer. Monotonic within a producer, so it also serves as the queue position and the daemon storage key. 26-char Crockford base32.

agent_id.description: A producer-assigned label like "claude-tab3" used to disambiguate which session is waiting.

session_started.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when the wrapped agent process was launched. Optional; omit or send the zero value if unknown.

prompt.description: The human-readable question, e.g. "Allow bash command `rm -rf node_modules`?".

context.description: The last ~20 lines of agent stdout for situational awareness. Truncated to 4096 bytes (MaxContextBytes) by the producer.

choices.description: The response options offered by the agent. At least one is required; exactly one SHOULD be marked is_default=true (matching the agent's own default), and every key MUST be unique within the array so answers resolve unambiguously.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the producer will give up waiting and abort the prompt. The daemon SHOULD NOT accept answers past this time. Optional.

key.description: The short identifier echoed back to the agent (e.g. "y", "n", "always"). MUST be unique among the envelope's choices.

label.description: The human-readable button text (e.g. "Approve", "Deny", "Approve and remember").

is_default.description: Marks the choice that fires on Enter or on expiry. The web UI highlights it and the wrapper picks it on timeout.
